topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various laws and regulations aimed at protecting sensitive personal information and ensuring accountability in the event of data breaches.

Document 1: Defines 'identity theft' to mean a fraud committed using the sensitive personal information of another individual

Document 2: Requires any federal agency or business entity engaged in interstate commerce that uses, accesses, transmits, stores, disposes of, or collects sensitive, personally identifiable information, following the discovery of a security breach, to notify any U.S. resident whose information may have been accessed or acquired.

Document 5: Imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to five years for intentionally or willfully concealing a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information

Document 1: The Department of the Interior may accept title to any real property located within the boundaries of the original 1857 reservation of the Confederated Tribes of the Grand Ronde Community of Oregon
Gaming is prohibited on such real property, except for real property within two miles of a specified gaming facility.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Energy to convey specified land identified as Parcel A to the Lovelace Respiratory Research Institute in New Mexico
Makes the Institute responsible for completing all environmental remediation required with respect to such parcel for all environmental conditions related to or arising from contamination.
Document 4: Amends federal law concerning the leasing to private entities by the Secretary of the Interior of certain federal oil shale reserves in Colorado

Requires amounts deposited into the Colorado special fund to be used to assist state agencies, school districts, and political subdivisions affected by the development and production of energy resources from oil shale.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to accept the offer, and on conveyance of such land, to simultaneously convey to the Company specified Forest Service land
Provides for the non-federal land to become part of BLM's Shoshone District and the boundaries of such District to be adjusted to include such land.
Document 6: Establishes in the Treasury the Trinity River Restoration Fund to be available to the Secretary of the Interior
Authorizes the Secretary to accept and expend funds from public and private sources to assist in the implementation of the Record of Decision.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 to double from 10 to 20 years the period of a term for grazing permits and leases for domestic livestock grazing on public lands
Excludes the renewal, reissuance, or transfer of a grazing permit or lease by the Secretary concerned from the requirement under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969.
